ucs.title: [LLM] Black Basta (Storm-1811 / UNC4393) Quick Assist social-engineering chain — IT-impersonation vishing leading to LOLBAS download
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Black Basta affiliate playbook publicly attributed by Microsoft to Storm-1811: email-bombing the user, calling via Teams while impersonating IT, then walking them through launching quickassist.exe so the operator can paste/run a curl/PowerShell stager that drops Qakbot/DarkGate/Pikabot, ScreenConnect, or a Cobalt Strike beacon. Pivot is parent=quickassist.exe spawning script/download interpreters or RMM installers — a chain virtually unique to Black Basta in 2024/2025 intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="quickassist.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="QuickAssist.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process_name,"(?i)^(powershell|pwsh|cmd|curl|certutil|bitsadmin|msiexec|rundll32|regsvr32|mshta|cscript|wscript|installutil|wmic)\.exe$") OR match(process,"(?i)(http(s)?://|-enc(odedcommand)?|downloadstring|iwr |invoke-webrequest|frombase64string|ScreenConnect\.|AnyDesk|TeamViewer_Setup|NetSupport)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process
ucs.kql:
let qa = DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "quickassist.exe" or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "quickassist.exe";
qa
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","msiexec.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","installutil.exe","wmic.exe")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-enc","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","FromBase64String","ScreenConnect","AnyDesk","NetSupport","TeamViewer_Setup","connectwisecontrol")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("screenconnect","connectwisecontrol","anydesk","netsupport","pastebin","transfer.sh")
    | project DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTimestamp=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| where isnull(NetTimestamp) or (NetTimestamp between (Timestamp - 10m .. Timestamp + 30m))
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Quick Assist (quickassist.exe) is rarely launched in enterprise environments and almost never spawns a download/script interpreter or RMM installer organically. Microsoft's May-2024 Storm-1811 advisory and Mandiant UNC4393 reporting both call out this exact chain (Teams vishing → Quick Assist → curl/PowerShell stager → ScreenConnect/Qakbot/DarkGate → Black Basta deployment), which differentiates i

ucs.title: [LLM] Black Basta (UNC4393) lateral deployment — remote service install + smbexec/PsExec admin-share writes preceding mass encryption
ucs.description: Hunts the post-CobaltStrike movement and ransomware staging stage uniquely characteristic of Black Basta operators: writing the encryptor binary to ADMIN$/C$ on multiple peers, registering it as a service via remote SCM (T1569.002), and chaining with vssadmin/wevtutil cleanup. The combination of cross-host SMB writes from a single source within minutes of new service creation referencing a freshly-dropped binary in C:\Windows\ or C:\ProgramData\ is the distinctive Black Basta detonation pattern documented by CISA AA24-131A.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count dc(Filesystem.dest) as victim_count values(Filesystem.dest) as victims values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ADMIN$\\*","*\\C$\\Windows\\*","*\\C$\\ProgramData\\*") AND Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" by Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.src | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where victim_count >= 3 | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="PsExec*.exe" OR Processes.process="*sc.exe* create *" OR Processes.process="*sc.exe*\\\\*binPath*" OR Processes.process="*vssadmin* delete shadows*" OR Processes.process="*wevtutil* cl *") by Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime user src victim_count victims files process_name process
ucs.kql:
let window = 30m;
let mass_writes = DeviceFileEvents
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\ADMIN$\", @"\C$\Windows\", @"\C$\ProgramData\")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
| summarize VictimCount=dcount(DeviceName), Victims=make_set(DeviceName,50), Files=make_set(FileName,20), FirstWrite=min(Timestamp), LastWrite=max(Timestamp)
    by InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, bin(Timestamp, window)
| where VictimCount >= 3;
let svc_or_cleanup = DeviceProcessEvents
| where (FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create" and ProcessCommandLine has "binPath")
    or (FileName in~ ("PsExec.exe","PsExec64.exe","paexec.exe","csexec.exe"))
    or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadows")
    or (FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "cl")
    or (FileName =~ "net.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("stop","start")  and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("backup","sql","veeam","exchange"))
| project SvcTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine;
mass_writes
| join kind=inner svc_or_cleanup on $left.InitiatingProcessAccountName == $right.AccountName
| where SvcTime between (FirstWrite - window .. LastWrite + window)
| project FirstWrite, LastWrite, InitiatingProcessAccountName, VictimCount, Victims, Files, DeviceName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, SvcTime
| order by FirstWrite desc
ucs.rationale: Black Basta / UNC4393 affiliates (post-Conti lineage) consistently stage their encryptor by pushing it to ADMIN$/C$ across multiple peers from a single beachhead account, then create remote services to run it — a sequence captured in CISA AA24-131A and Mandiant's UNC4393 profile. Tying mass cross-host SMB writes to the same actor identity that runs sc.exe create / PsExec / vssadmin / wevtutil with
